W1-S1.implementation: Added npm workspaces monorepo: apps/web (Next.js 15 + Tailwind + App Router), packages/core (schemas home), packages/ai (stub), packages/content (fixtures). Root tooling: package.json workspaces, tsconfig, ESLint/Prettier, Vitest config, tests/smoke.test.ts, README, .nvmrc. No game logic in web yet.
W1-S1.relevance: Establishes the physical repo layout and CI-friendly scripts (test/typecheck/lint/build) required before any WorldDefinition or runtime work. All later packages depend on this skeleton.
W1-S1.future: Enables W1-S2–S16 schema work in packages/core; Phase 1 apps/web world-play UI; packages/ai gateway in Phase 2; content worlds in packages/content; Future_Features/ quest generator and 2D layers attach to same workspace without restructuring.
W1-S1.tests: tests/smoke.test.ts (1 test). Verified 2026-05-28: npm test, npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm run build, npm run dev (shell only).
W1-S1.evidence: Repo skeleton verified; smoke test passes.

W1-S2.implementation: Created packages/core/src/schemas/worldDna.ts and safetyMode.ts with Zod enums (genre, tone, sessionLengthMinutes, coreLoop, consequenceIntensity, aiCreativity, safetyMode teen|adult only). Exported types and parse/safeParse helpers. Examples: world-dna-teen.example.json, world-dna-adult.example.json.
W1-S2.relevance: WorldDNA is the tone/safety/loop contract for every world including Stonepass. Teen/adult-only safetyMode is a hard product rule from §4.
W1-S2.future: Phase 5 WorldArchitectAgent generates WorldDNA from prompts; creator cockpit DNA editor (W12-S1); remix may patch DNA fields; all generated worlds must validate against this schema before save.
W1-S2.tests: packages/core/tests/unit/schemas/worldDna.test.ts (11 tests). Example JSON validation included. Suite at step end: 97 tests passing.

W1-S3.implementation: Created packages/core/src/schemas/playerChoice.ts: id, label, optional description, requiredFlags, blockedByFlags, consequenceId (required). FlagIdSchema reused by beats and ledger.
W1-S3.relevance: Every player action in the text runtime resolves through PlayerChoice → consequenceId. Stonepass ogre bridge needs five distinct choice objects.
W1-S3.future: Phase 1 choice resolver (W2-S4); conditional routes and replay variation; AI must not invent choice shapes; playtester checks no-op choices (W11-S5).
W1-S3.evidence: Choices validate with deterministic consequence references.

W1-S4.implementation: Created packages/core/src/schemas/storyBeat.ts composing PlayerChoiceSchema: trigger, availableChoices (min 1), possibleConsequences, optional requiredFlags/blockedByFlags, isEnding, isHidden.
W1-S4.relevance: Story beats are the graph nodes of the world engine. Ogre bridge is the first Stonepass beat; later beats gate on flags from consequences.
W1-S4.future: W2-S3 beat selector; Phase 3 instance beats; graph generator W8-S1–S5; validateWorldDefinition reachability (W1-S14); Director select_next_beat (Phase 2).
W1-S4.evidence: StoryBeat validates only with playable structure (choices + possibleConsequences).

W1-S5.implementation: Created packages/core/src/schemas/consequence.ts per v4.2 hybrid: addFlags, removeFlags, unlockGoals, completeGoals, exposeLocations, closeLocations, visibleChanges, structured npcUpdates[], temporaryInstances[]. Example consequence-fight-ogre.example.json.
W1-S5.relevance: Consequence Engine (Phase 1) applies these objects to WorldLedger. Fight-ogre path sets landslide_triggered and starts cave instance reference.
W1-S5.future: W2-S5/W3-S1 consequence application; temporary instance spawn from temporaryInstances[]; NPC attitude updates; health score consequence quality (W6); generated consequences must validate here.
W1-S5.tests: packages/core/tests/unit/schemas/consequence.test.ts (8 tests). v4.2 field names per FULL_CURSOR §22.
W1-S5.evidence: Consequence validates; fight-ogre example documents flagship flags.

W1-S6.implementation: Created packages/core/src/schemas/worldLedger.ts: WorldEventSchema + WorldLedgerSchema with activeFlags, resolvedFlags, unlockedGoals, completedGoals, discoveredLocations, worldEvents. Helper createEmptyWorldLedger().
W1-S6.relevance: World memory for Stonepass: ogre choice must appear as flags, goals, locations, and typed worldEvents. Session.ledger embeds this schema (W1-S11).
W1-S6.future: W3-S3 flag lifecycle; ledger debug UI W3-S4; save/load sessions W9-S5; playtester missing-goal detection W11-S4; share/fork snapshots include ledger state.
W1-S6.evidence: Empty and post-ogre ledgers validate; v4.2 names (worldEvents not events).

W1-S7.implementation: Created packages/core/src/schemas/directorDecision.ts with v4.1 action enum (select_next_beat, generate_consequence, generate_instance, generate_npc_reaction, summarize_world, suggest_session_wrapup), targetId, reason, confidence 0–1, optional safetyNotes.
W1-S7.relevance: Structured output contract for AI Director before any provider exists. AI proposes; engine executes—Director never mutates ledger directly.
W1-S7.future: W1-S16 FakeProvider returns DirectorDecision; W4-S4 DirectorAgent; AIResult wraps suggestions (W1-S13); debug panel W4-S7; author-time beat suggestions with validation.
W1-S7.evidence: Only canonical Director actions pass schema.

W1-S8.implementation: Created packages/core/src/schemas/temporaryInstance.ts and TemporaryInstanceRoomSchema: requiredEntryFlags, completionConsequenceId, cleanupBehavior (+ remain_inactive), optional description, connectedRoomIds on rooms.
W1-S8.relevance: Stonepass proof chain: landslide exposes instance_hidden_cave with rooms, encounter, puzzle, collapse cleanup.
W1-S8.future: Phase 3 W5-S1–S7 instance runtime, room navigation, encounters, puzzles; 2D/3D can render same room graph later; generated instances must validate before play.
W1-S8.evidence: Cave-shaped instance validates before Phase 3 runtime.

W1-S9.implementation: Created packages/core/src/schemas/npc.ts: id, name, role, description, optional attitude (merged enum incl. trusting/fearful), toneRules, knownFlags.
W1-S9.relevance: Ogre and elder profiles for Stonepass dialogue tone and NPC memory. consequence.npcUpdates references npcId.
W1-S9.future: W4-S5 NPCReactionAgent; Stonepass showcase elder/ogre arcs; public UGC needs toneRules + safetyMode; Future_Features regional quest NPCs use same contract.
W1-S9.evidence: NPC profiles validate for Stonepass content authoring.

W1-S10.implementation: Created packages/core/src/schemas/worldDefinition.ts + schemaVersion.ts (CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION 0.2.0). Composes WorldDNA, storyBeats, consequences, npcs, optional temporaryInstances. createWorldDefinitionShell() helper. Example world-definition-stonepass-minimal.example.json (composition demo only).
W1-S10.relevance: Single loadable world object for Stonepass and all official/generated worlds. Shape validation only until W1-S14 cross-file checks.
W1-S10.future: W1-S15 canonical world file; W2-S1 loader; W7-S5 generated worlds; fork/remix W10; health score W6; 2D/3D read same WorldDefinition.
W1-S10.tests: packages/core/tests/unit/schemas/worldDefinition.test.ts (10 tests). Minimal Stonepass-like fixture passes at schemaVersion 0.2.0.
W1-S10.evidence: WorldDefinition composes all Phase 0 contracts; graph validation deferred to W1-S14.

W1-S11.implementation: Created packages/core/src/schemas/worldSession.ts (currentBeatId, ledger, turnNumber, choiceHistory, optional activeTemporaryInstanceId/currentTemporaryRoomId, debugEvents). Created debugEvent.ts (§9) for composition. createWorldSession(), parse/safeParse. Example world-session-stonepass-start.example.json.
W1-S11.relevance: Canonical save-state: not React local state. Every play session ties to worldId/worldVersionId and v4.2 ledger.
W1-S11.future: W2-S2 session init; W9 save/load; Phase 3 instance fields; debug trace UI; multiplayer would need session sync later (out of MVP).
W1-S11.tests: packages/core/tests/unit/schemas/worldSession.test.ts (11 tests). npm test: 108 passed after step.
W1-S11.evidence: New session parses; bad ledger/beat/turn rejected.

W1-S12.implementation: Extended W1-S11 debugEvent.ts with dedicated tests. Added packages/core/src/debug/appendDebugEvent.ts (immutable session append). Examples: debug-event-choice-selected, debug-event-consequence-applied, debug-event-fallback-used.
W1-S12.relevance: Explains why state changed (choice, consequence, fallback, validation_failed). Required for creator/debug panels and AI failure transparency.
W1-S12.future: W3-S5 runtime logs every transition; W4-S6 fallback_used events; W4-S7 reasoning panel; playtest reports cite debug trail.
W1-S12.tests: debugEvent.test.ts (8 tests); appendDebugEvent.test.ts (2 tests). npm test: 118 passed.
W1-S12.evidence: DebugEvent available for runtime and UI traces; append helper exported.

W1-S13.implementation: Created packages/core/src/schemas/aiResult.ts: AIResultSchema, createAIResultSchema(valueSchema) for typed value (e.g. DirectorDecision), parse/safeParse/parseTypedAIResult. Examples: ai-result-success-director, ai-result-validation-failure.
W1-S13.relevance: Wrapper for all AI Gateway responses: ok, provider, fallbackUsed, validationErrors, raw, latencyMs, generationSeed. Enforces AI proposes / validators check / engine executes.
W1-S13.future: W1-S16 FakeProvider returns AIResult; W4-S1 Gateway; all agents (Director, NPC, Architect, Playtester, Critic); never pass raw model JSON into gameplay.
W1-S13.evidence: Success and failure AIResult objects validate; typed Director wrapper works.

W1-S14.implementation: Created packages/core/src/validators/validateWorldDefinition.ts and parseAndValidateWorldDefinition(): reference integrity, duplicate IDs, flag vocabulary, reachability fixpoint from startingBeatId, dead-end choice checks. Exported from @playable-worlds/core.
W1-S14.relevance: Second validation layer (§11). Prevents broken Stonepass/graph worlds from reaching runtime or generation pipelines.
W1-S14.future: W1-S15 Stonepass must pass; W2-S1 loader calls this; W7-S5 generated worlds; W6 health score reuses checks; W11 playtester; share/fork blocked on invalid worlds.
W1-S14.evidence: Whole-world validation exists; broken fixtures fail with error paths.

W1-S15.implementation: Created packages/content/worlds/stonepass/stonepass-valley.world.json (schemaVersion 0.2.0): 5 ogre beats/choices with fully authored consequences, post-ogre beats, hidden cave TemporaryInstance, ogre+elder NPCs, fight→landslide→cave→dragon_awake proof chain. stonepassWorld.test.ts (3 tests).
W1-S15.relevance: Canonical authored Stonepass data for W2-S1 loader and Phase 1–3 proof playthrough.
W1-S15.future: W2-S7 path tests; W5 cave chain; W6 end-to-end Stonepass; W7-S10 library seed; Mosswood quest via W8-S9–S12.
W1-S15.tests: packages/core/tests/unit/content/stonepassWorld.test.ts (3 tests). Full suite 2026-05-28: npm test 145+ passed.
W1-S15.evidence: Canonical Stonepass v1 world passes parseAndValidateWorldDefinition.

W2-S1.implementation: packages/core/src/world/loadWorld.ts (loadWorldFromFile, loadWorld, isKnownWorldId); packages/content/src/paths.ts. Unit + integration tests.
W2-S1.relevance: First Phase 1 runtime piece; loads validated WorldDefinition from disk.
W2-S1.future: W2-S2 session init; W2-S4–S7 runtime; W7 generated worlds use same loader.
W2-S1.evidence: Stonepass loads via loadWorld without throwing on validation failures.

W2-S2.relevance: Bridges loaded world to playable session state.
W2-S2.evidence: Fresh session at startingBeatId with turn 0 and empty ledger.

W2-S3.relevance: Presents beat content; gates beats by ledger flags.
W2-S3.future: W2-S4 choice resolver; W4-S4 Director select_next_beat.

W2-S4.relevance: Deterministic player input gate before consequence application.
W2-S4.future: W2-S5 apply consequence; W2-S6 text play UI.
W2-S4.evidence: All five ogre bridge choices resolve; fake choice blocked.

W2-S5.relevance: First remembered world change from player choice; completes core engine loop.
W2-S5.future: W2-S6 text play UI; Phase 3 temporaryInstances/npcUpdates deferred.
W2-S5.evidence: fight_ogre updates flags, goals, locations, worldEvents, debug trace.

W2-S6.implementation: PLANNED (Status Next): apps/web/features/world-play — minimal text UI wired to @playable-worlds/core runtime.
W2-S6.relevance: First human-playable Stonepass proof in browser.
W2-S6.future: W2-S7 path tests; W3 world-play expansion.
W2-S6.tests: PLANNED: web smoke test for play page and valid choice buttons.

W7-S7.implementation: PLANNED: packages/core/schemas/library — CreatureTemplate, NpcArchetype, EncounterTemplate, PuzzleTemplate Zod schemas.
W7-S7.relevance: Tagged reusable content for themed worlds (lava/ocean/machine).

W8-S6.implementation: PLANNED: packages/core/schemas/worldBlueprint — player knobs (theme, beat outline, difficulty, reward bounds).
W8-S6.relevance: High-level player controls for generated worlds.
W8-S6.future: W8-S7–S8 Architect wiring; W7-S6 preview extension.

W8-S9.relevance: Per-quest creator contract distinct from play-time AI flavor.
